Coverage: 360 KM - FL 500. Azimuth unusable in sector 340°-030° below 5000 FT.
Usable: Up to 17 NM in the area 035°(L)/035°(R) in relation to RCL. From 17 NM to 25 NM in the area 010°(L)/010°(R) in relation to RCL.
Usable: Up to 17 NM in the area 035°(L)/035°(R) in relation to RCL. From 17 NM to 25 NM in the area 010°(L)/010°(R) in relation to RCL.
Usable up to 15 NM BTN 8°(L) and 8°(R) of RCL
Usable up to 15 NM BTN 8°(L) and 8°(R) of RCL
Services are available O/R. Request to be submitted to the AD not later than 1500 (1400) UTC.
Geoid undulation at AD ELEV PSN is 12 M
Secondary power supply to all lighting at AD. Switch-over time: 1 SEC.
ARP site at AD: 258°/1075 M from THR 09L. Direction and distance from (city): 045°, 9 KM from Donlon.

aixm:theOrganisationAuthority.xlink:title: DONLON DOWNTOWN HELIPORT AUTHORITY EA-EADH ORG

aixm:LinguisticNote.aixm:note: Geoid undulation at ELEV PSN is 9 M
Heliport reference point site at heliport: geometric centre of TLOF. Direction and distance from (city): Donlon downtown, east shore of Donlon river.
During these hours the aerodrome is available O/R with at least 3 hours prior notice sent to AD office
During these hours the aerodrome is available O/R with at least 2 hours prior notice sent to AD office

Circle centered on DONLON DOWNTOWN HELIPORT (EADH) ARP
Rules of the air not always complied with. Controlled FLT separated from training flights by ACC Amswell. Non-controlled flights call Amswell information on 121.100MHZ for information on ACT.
Rules of the air not always complied with. Controlled FLT separated from training flights by ACC Amswell. Non-controlled flights call Amswell information on 121.100MHZ for information on ACT.
Rules of the air not always complied with. Controlled FLT separated from training flights by ACC Amswell. Non-controlled flights call Amswell information on 121.100MHZ for information on ACT.
Radiosonde/Upperwind radio observations. MAX LEN 30 M. MAX WT 2.3 KG. MAX ROC 1400 FT/MIN.
EET 80 MIN. Subject to ATC clearance.
In eruptions risk of volcanic ash up to 10000 M. Avoid flying below 11000M.
TAMALS last erupted August 2000 and is considered active.
The area will not be allocated for altitudes above cloud base (base for CU clouds). Allocation of the area may take place only after 1100 (1000).

APP and FATO lighting: FATO area edge, air TWY to apron

Non-scheduled public air traffic with helicopters is permitted only after prior approval from the Donlon Aerodrome Administration. Any contact concerning the above shall be made via the handling company or directly to the airport office during the hours of service and, if possible, not later than the day before the flight is to be carried out.
